mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals monetary easing, reducing discount rates for equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain negative in 60%+ of periods when FEDFUNDS declines by >0.25pp over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 100k jobs/month signals robust labor market demand, pushing equity prices higher through corporate revenue optimism over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns fall below 0.5% in 75%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in housing starts and consumer price inflation signal weak demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as recession risks rise.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns exceed 2% in 50%+ of periods with simultaneous HOUST < 900k and CPIAUCSL < 225.

mechanism_narrative: Manufacturing output declines below 95% of 2017 levels indicate supply chain disruptions, causing equity market sell-offs through corporate profit erosion over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain positive in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO falls below 95.

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5pp+ increase in unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions, triggering equity sell-offs through reduced consumer spending over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns exceed 1% in 50%+ of periods with UNRATE increases >0.5pp.

mechanism_narrative: Strong consumer spending (RSAFS > $400B) combined with rising housing starts (HOUST > 950k) indicates robust demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns fall below 0.5% in 75%+ of instances with RSAFS > 400B and HOUST > 950k.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% combined with stagnant monetary policy (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) creates uncertainty, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns exceed 2% in 50%+ of periods with CPIAUCSL >2.5 and FEDFUNDS <0.25.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth below 1% signals weak supply-side momentum, causing equity market declines through corporate earnings disappointments over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain positive in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO growth falls below 1%.
